commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(model-3d): add face groups for unified outer frame selection and push-pull

After polygon split, outer strip faces are now registered as a face group.
Clicking any strip highlights the entire frame; push-pulling extrudes all
strips together. Adds extrudeFaceGroup() for coordinated group extrusion
with proper wall creation only on the group boundary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(model-3d): merge outer faces after polygon split for clean strips

Add mergeFaces() to HEMesh and post-split merge to splitFaceByPolygon.
A rectangle on a face now produces 5 faces (1 inner + 4 strips) instead
of 9 (3x3 grid). Each strip borders exactly one inner edge, giving
SketchUp-like behavior where the inner rectangle and surrounding strips
are individually push-pullable.

commit.message:
fix: replace execSync with async exec in OpenClaw plugin hooks

The before_prompt_build hook and session event handlers used execSync
to run the skmemory CLI, which blocked the Node.js event loop for up
to 30 seconds. During that time ALL tool calls failed with "Tool not
found" because the event loop was frozen.

Fix:
- Add runSKMemoryAsync() using child_process.exec (non-blocking)
- before_prompt_build now fire-and-forgets cache refresh, serves stale
- session:start/resume use async CLI calls
- Pre-warm rehydration cache at plugin init (non-blocking)
- Sync execSync retained only for explicit tool execute handlers
  where the caller is already awaiting the result

Also adds SKCAPSTONE_AGENT env var and skenv PATH to all CLI calls,
shell argument escaping, and bumps plugin version to 0.6.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redirect scan orchestrator display output to stderr

scan_blob_containers() and scan_file_shares() are called inside $()
command substitution, which captures ALL stdout into a variable.
This swallowed all progress indicators, info messages, and per-container
summaries — making Phase 1 appear frozen with no visible output.

Fix: add >&2 to all print_info, print_warn, printf, and echo display
lines so they go to the terminal (stderr), while only the final JSON
inventory goes to stdout for $() capture.

commit.message:
fix: Partners page auth — use cookie auth instead of deleted Bearer token

AuthContext clears localStorage auth_token on load, so getToken()
always returns null. All fetch functions returned early with isLoading
stuck at true (infinite spinner).

Fix: Replace Bearer token auth with credentials: 'same-origin'
(cookie auth) + CSRF tokens for mutations.

Also: Remove hardcoded rule counts from healing tier labels.

commit.message:
Run first 5 experiments: optimized Riegel exponent to 1.12

Experiments:
1. Pure Riegel (1.06) → MAE 15.0m (matched baseline)
2. Riegel exp 1.10 → MAE 11.5m (big improvement)
3. Riegel exp 1.12 → MAE 10.9m (best so far)
4. Riegel exp 1.14 → MAE 11.5m (overshot)
5. Riegel 1.12 + mileage adj → MAE 11.5m (mileage hurts)

Best: Riegel 1.12 → MAE 10.9 min (27% improvement over 1.06 baseline)
Key insight: standard 1.06 exponent systematically underpredicts
recreational runners; 1.12 corrects for their greater fatigue in marathons.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: parse date-only strings as local time in fmtDate

new Date("2026-03-20") parses as UTC midnight, showing the previous day
in negative timezone offsets. Parse date-only strings using local year/month/day.

commit.message:
kind-pasteur-S40: THM-098 Pfaffian-Betti separation + S-phase characterization

THM-098: At n=6, |Pf(S)| perfectly separates topological phases:
  C-phase (beta_1>0): |Pf| in {1,3}
  S-phase (beta_3>0): |Pf| in {7,9}
  Zero overlap — exact theorem

Algebraic mechanism: eigenvalue triples (a,b,c) lie on sphere a^2+b^2+c^2=15.
Only 5 achievable triples. C-phase = anisotropic (small abc), S-phase = degenerate (large abc).

S-phase at n=6 completely characterized (320 tournaments):
  Type 1: score (1,1,1,4,4,4), c3=2, c5=0, H=9, |Pf|=9 (80 tours)
  Type 2: score (2,2,2,3,3,3), c3=8, c5=12, H=45=max, |Pf|=7 (240 tours)
  ALL have alpha(Omega_3)=2 (one vertex-disjoint 3-cycle pair)

The H-maximizers at n=6 are ALL S-phase! beta_3>0 iff H-maximizer among regular.

commit.message:
feat: S4Claw 添加前台服务和通知栏通知

功能:
- 无障碍服务启动时自动启动前台服务
- 通知栏显示 "S4Claw 正在运行"
- 通知内容: "屏幕观察 · 设备控制"
- 服务关闭时自动停止前台服务

技术实现:
- 在 PhoneAccessibilityService.onServiceConnected() 启动前台服务
- 在 PhoneAccessibilityService.onDestroy() 停止前台服务
- 使用 ForegroundService 显示持久通知
- 通知优先级: LOW，不打扰用户

通知渠道:
- ID: s4claw_service
- 名称: S4Claw 服务
- 描述: S4Claw 正在提供屏幕观察和设备控制功能

用户体验:
- 用户可以看到 S4Claw 正在运行
- 点击通知可打开 S4Claw 应用
- 通知常驻，明确服务状态

commit.message:
Redesign forgotten PIN flow as a clean 2-step wizard

Replace the janky inline recovery form (which showed below the sign-in form
simultaneously) with a proper 2-step flow that takes over the panel:

- Move #account-recovery-box outside #account-auth-signedout so the sign-in
  form hides completely when recovery is active
- Step 1: name + email + Send Code button
- Step 2 (auto-advances on success): "Code sent to {email}" hint, 6-digit
  code input, new PIN input, Set New PIN button; Resend code link returns to step 1
- Forgot PIN? becomes a subtle text link instead of a third action button
- setAccountRecoveryOpen() now hides/shows the sign-in form and clears all
  inputs on close; new setRecoveryStep() drives step transitions
- startPinRecovery() reads from dedicated recovery name input and advances to
  step 2 on success; completePinRecovery() does the same
- Add Resend code listener; Back to sign in clears status quietly
- New CSS: .recovery-header, .recovery-title, .recovery-step-label,
  .recovery-email-hint, .recovery-back-link, .btn-link

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
